text: AMRITA InCTF 2026 - Official Rules & Terms
OPERATION VAJRA

Welcome to Amrita InCTF 2026 - Operation VAJRA, India's premier national-level ethical hacking Capture The Flag (CTF) competition, conducted by Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham in collaboration with team bi0s. By registering for, accessing, or participating in InCTF 2026 in any capacity, you agree to be legally bound by these Terms & Conditions. Please read this document carefully before completing your registration.
1. About the Event

Amrita InCTF 2026 ("InCTF" or "the Event") is a multi-month cybersecurity Capture The Flag contest. Themed as Operation VAJRA, it is a high-stakes cyber defence simulation designed to identify, train, and elevate India's next generation of security specialists. The competition tests technical proficiency under simulated high-pressure environments, built on the principles of national resilience.

The Event is hosted by Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham and organized in collaboration with team bi0s. It has been operational continuously since 2010 and is recognized as India's premier ethical hacking contest for students.
2. Eligibility
Participation in InCTF 2026 is open to the following categories of individuals:
Undergraduate students currently enrolled in their 1st, 2nd, 3rd, or 4th year of study
Students from relevant disciplines including Computer Science, Information Technology, Artificial Intelligence, Cyber Security, Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE), and related fields
Cybersecurity-interested professionals from the industry who meet the contest criteria

Teams must consist of 1 to 5 members. All team members must independently satisfy the eligibility criteria. Providing false information about eligibility during registration may result in immediate disqualification without refund.
3. Registration & Participation

To participate in InCTF 2026, all teams must complete the official registration process through the designated Mission Control portal at register.inctf.in. Registration constitutes your acceptance of these Terms & Conditions in full.
Each participant must register using accurate personal and institutional information
Each team must have a minimum of 1 and a maximum of 5 members at the time of registration
Duplicate or fraudulent registrations will be cancelled without notice or refund
Participants are responsible for ensuring that their registration details remain current and accurate throughout the competition period
Organizers reserve the right to verify the eligibility and identity of any participant or team at any stage of the competition.
4. Payment Terms
4.1 Registration Fee

A registration fee may be applicable for participation in InCTF 2026. The applicable fee, payment modes, and deadlines will be communicated through the official event portal at register.inctf.in and via official communication channel.
4.2 No-Refund Policy

ALL REGISTRATION FEES ARE STRICTLY NON-REFUNDABLE. Once a registration has been submitted and payment has been processed, no refunds will be issued under any circumstances, including but not limited to:
Voluntary withdrawal or team dissolution after payment
Failure to meet eligibility requirements discovered after registration
Inability to attend or participate due to personal, academic, technical, or professional reasons
Changes in event format, schedule, or delivery mode
Disqualification due to violation of the rules or code of conduct
Force majeure events, including but not limited to natural disasters, government-imposed restrictions, or technical outages
Note: By completing the payment process, participants and their guardians (where applicable) expressly acknowledge and accept this no-refund policy.
4.3 Payment Disputes

Any disputes regarding payment processing errors (e.g., double charges, failed transactions) must be reported to the organizing team within 7 days of the transaction. Disputes raised after this window will not be entertained. Refunds will only be considered in verified cases of technical payment failure where the registration was not successfully recorded.
4.4 Prizes

A prize pool worth INR 5 Lakhs (subject to change) will be awarded to top-performing categories of winners during the Final Deployment phase. Prizes are non-transferable and may be subject to applicable tax deductions as per Indian law. The organizers reserve the right to modify prize structures at their discretion with prior notice.
5. Code of Conduct

All participants are expected to uphold the highest standards of integrity and ethical behavior throughout the competition. Violations of the code of conduct may result in immediate disqualification without refund.
Participants must not engage in any activity that could compromise the integrity of the competition infrastructure or other participants' systems
Attacks on the competition platform, scoreboard, or other teams' infrastructure outside of designated challenge environments are strictly prohibited
Flag sharing, collusion between teams, or any form of cheating is forbidden
Discrimination, harassment, or abusive conduct toward other participants, organizers, or volunteers will not be tolerated
Participants must comply with all applicable laws and regulations, including the Information Technology Act, 2000 and its amendments
Social engineering attacks targeting competition staff or other participants are prohibited
6. Intellectual Property

All challenge content, materials, problems, and associated resources provided as part of InCTF 2026 are the exclusive intellectual property of Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham and/or team bi0s. Participants are granted a limited, non-exclusive license to use such materials solely for the purpose of competing in InCTF 2026.
Participants may not reproduce, distribute, publish, or publicly disclose challenge content, flags, or solutions during the course of the event
Write-ups and solutions may only be shared publicly after the official conclusion of the relevant competition phase, as announced by the organizers
Submissions made by participants during the competition may be used by the organizers for educational and promotional purposes
7. Data Privacy

By registering, participants consent to the collection, storage, and processing of their personal information (including name, email, institution, and performance data) for the purpose of operating and administering InCTF 2026. This data may also be used for:
Communication of event updates, schedules, and announcements
Certificate and prize distribution
Identification and recommendation for internship and hiring opportunities (with participant consent)
Statistical reporting and event promotion
Participant data will not be sold to third parties. Sponsors and partner organizations may receive aggregated data. Data handling is governed by applicable Indian data protection laws.
8. Limitation of Liability
Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, team bi0s, their employees, volunteers, and affiliates shall not be liable for:
Any loss of data, system damage, or disruption caused during participation
Technical difficulties, connectivity issues, or platform downtime
Any indirect, consequential, or incidental loss arising from participation in InCTF 2026
Decisions made by sponsors or partner organizations regarding hiring or internship offers

Participants assume all risks associated with participation in a cybersecurity competition environment and acknowledge that the organizers make no warranties, express or implied, regarding the outcomes of participation.
9. Modifications to Terms

The organizers reserve the right to amend, update, or modify these Terms & Conditions at any time. Participants will be notified of material changes through the official event portal and registered email addresses. Continued participation following such notification constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.
10. Disqualification & Sanctions

The organizing team retains sole discretion to disqualify any participant or team found to be in violation of these Terms & Conditions or the event rules. Disqualified teams forfeit all prizes, awards, and registration fees without recourse. Decisions made by the organizing committee regarding disqualification are final and binding.
11. Governing Law & Jurisdiction

These Terms & Conditions shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from or in connection with InCTF 2026 shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India.
12. Contact Information
For queries related to registration, payment, or these Terms & Conditions, please contact the organizing team through the official channels:
Website: www.inctf.in
Discord: https://discord.gg/pJWzURVMMH (Join via the link on the official website)
Mission Control Portal: inctf.in/mission-control
For technical issues related to payment, please include your registration ID including transaction order ID and reference in all communications.
By registering for Amrita InCTF 2026 - Operation VAJRA, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agreed to these Terms & Conditions in their entirety.
© 2026 Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham | InCTF. All Rights Reserved.
